summary.quantification_method: label-free quantification with spike-in internal standard

matched_class_path: gene modification or gain of function
primary_variables.matched_definition: Addition of bases or sequence cassette (for example donor insertion, tag insertion, transposon insertion).
evidence: Quantitative proteomic analysis through our chemical enrichment workflow identified specific 5-Hyl substrate proteins mediated by the overexpression of Jumonji-domain containing protein 6 (JMJD6).
293T cells expressing either control plasmids or plasmids encoding either the long or short form of JMJD6 were lysed for nuclear extraction

attributes.agent: JMJD6 (long form and short form splicing isoforms)
evidence: Cells were seeded in a 15 cm plate overnight and were then transfected with 1 ml of premixed medium containing 45 μg polyethyleneimine (PEI) (Sigma, St. Louis, MO) and 20 μg plasmid for 48 hours.
overexpression of the JMJD6 short form and long form

replicate_hints: Biological triplicate analysis was performed for each treatment condition in quantification.

evidence: The volcano plot analysis identified 283 sites... that were significantly upregulated with the overexpression of the JMJD6 short form
The volcano plot analysis identified... 303 sites that were significantly upregulated with the overexpression of the JMJD6... long form
Quantitative comparison of 5-Hyl abundance from the expression of long or short JMJD6 isoform identified a group of 108 sites

quantification_strategy: label-free quantification with spike-in internal standard

base_candidates_ranked.reason: The study uses label-free quantification (LFQ) with a spike-in internal standard (leupeptin) and DDA acquisition on an Orbitrap Fusion Lumos.

modifier_candidates_ranked.reason: The experiment uses a highly specialized chemical proteomics workflow involving periodate oxidation of lysine 5-hydroxylation (5-Hyl), hydrazide resin enrichment, and methoxyamine elution.
